# Human Review Checklist: [output type]

<!-- Attach one of these to any skill whose output affects money, legal rights, health,
     safety, reputation, or public claims. The reviewer is named by role, not "someone". -->

## What Is Being Reviewed

Artifact path/link, producing skill, date, and the AI session or run that produced it.

## Reviewer

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Required reviewer role | e.g., CPA, attorney, tech lead, PM/PO |
| Named reviewer | |
| Review date | |

## Provenance Check

- [ ] Every factual figure traces to a named source (file, tool output, or citation)
- [ ] Citations point to primary sources where required (statute, Rev. Proc., official filing)
- [ ] Assumptions are explicitly labeled and reasonable
- [ ] Perishable facts (dates, versions, thresholds) are current

## Content Check

- [ ] Claims are within the skill's allowed output class (leads/signals/options — not conclusions of law, liability, diagnosis, or guaranteed outcomes)
- [ ] Alternative/boring explanations considered where the output flags an anomaly
- [ ] Uncertainty is stated where it exists, with the right weight
- [ ] Nothing prohibited by the skill's Safety and Compliance section appears

## Consequence Check

- [ ] Worst plausible reader/user action based on this output has been considered
- [ ] Required limitations/disclaimers present and specific (not boilerplate)
- [ ] Escalation was used where the skill required it

## Verdict

**APPROVE / APPROVE WITH CHANGES / REJECT** — with reasons.
Changes required:

## Record

Where this completed checklist is stored (e.g., `reports/reviews/`), so approvals are auditable.
